title: Experimental Study of Crushed Granular Materials by the Notion of Fractal Dimension in 2D and 3D.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies crushing of local granular materials (limestone and shale) under Proctor, Los Angeles, and Micro-Deval tests, using fractal dimension as a quantitative descriptor of grain fragmentation and surface roughness. It compares 2D image-based fractal measures (Box Counting) with a 3D morphological measure (Blanket) and a sample-scale Masses method, finding that crushing increases with compaction energy and water sensitivity is much stronger for shale than limestone. The 3D method is argued to better capture the full grain microstructure than a single 2D projection.
measurement_types: 2D grain image analysis (Box Counting fractal dimension)

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include SEM micrographs of limestone and shale, photographs of individual colored control grains before and after mechanical tests, and the physical test outputs from Proctor, Los Angeles, and Micro-Deval experiments. The paper also states that granulometric analyses were performed before and after each test, implying raw sieve/mass measurements underlying the particle size distributions.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include Proctor compaction curves, particle size distribution curves before/after tests, and log-log regression plots used to extract fractal dimensions by the Masses, Box Counting, and Blanket methods. The paper reports fractal dimensions and correlation trends derived from these processed datasets.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement is given in the provided text. The paper only states that MATLAB R2009b was used to facilitate fractal-dimension calculations and that tables/figures summarize mineralogical, chemical, geotechnical, and test results.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only indirectly: SEM images, photographs of grains before/after tests, and laboratory test measurements from Proctor, Los Angeles, and Micro-Deval tests. No file formats, repository, or downloadable raw-data package are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include particle size distribution curves, Proctor curves, tabulated mineralogical/chemical/geotechnical properties, and calculated fractal dimensions (FDFR, 2D Box Counting FD, 3D Blanket FD). These are presented in figures and tables rather than as a separate dataset.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are not mentioned as being provided separately. The paper presents figures only; no statement about source-data files for individual figures is included.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: MATLAB R2009b is mentioned as the software used to facilitate calculation of the fractal dimensions, but no scripts, code, or notebooks are described or provided.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper provides plotted results and some tabulated values, but not the underlying numerical source data, raw image files, or analysis scripts needed to reproduce all figures directly.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily experimental and figure-driven. The key semantic distinction is that the raw inputs are laboratory measurements and images, while the published outputs are derived fractal dimensions and distribution curves. The paper emphasizes comparison between 2D projection-based and 3D morphology-based analysis, but does not provide a structured data archive.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods describe SEM imaging, photographs of control grains before and after tests, and laboratory mechanical tests (Proctor, Los Angeles, Micro-Deval) plus granulometric analysis.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: No explicit source-data repository or supplementary source-data files are mentioned; only figures and tables are shown in the paper.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: "MATLAB R2009b software was used to facilitate the calculation of the fractal dimension"; "Photos were taken before and after the tests"; "A granulometric analysis is carried out for the entire sample before and after each test"; figures and tables summarize results.
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not infer a separate downloadable dataset or source-data package. The paper contains experimental measurements and derived plots, but no explicit data-sharing statement, no raw file formats, and no scripts. The 3D Blanket and 2D Box Counting values are derived from images; the Masses-method values are derived from particle-size distributions.
